questions.question: The regent who served as Akbar's guardian from 1556 to 1560.
The fort where Akbar was born in 1542 in present-day Sind.
The place in Punjab where Akbar's coronation took place in 1556.
The general of Adil Shah who seized Delhi and Agra and was defeated in the Second Battle of Panipat.
The first Rajput chief to enter into a matrimonial alliance with Akbar.
The brave woman who defended Ahmadnagar against Akbar's forces.
The Mughal general who led the forces at the Battle of Haldighati in 1576.
The new religious path suggested by Akbar meaning 'religion of one God'.
The official who was in charge of land revenue and assessment under Akbar.
The capital city built by Akbar near Agra, named after the Sufi saint Salim Chisti.
The highest gateway in India, built at Fatehpur Sikri to commemorate Akbar's victory over Gujarat.
The official biographical account of Akbar's life written by Abul Fazl.
The famous musician in Akbar's court who composed Raag Darbari and Deepak Raag.
The revenue system introduced by Akbar, also known as the zabti system.
The Hall of Worship built by Akbar for religious discussions with scholars of different faiths.
